text: Clean code is code that is easy for others to read and understand. One of the best ways to make your code readable is to follow good naming conventions. For example, variable and function names should clearly describe what they do, like `calculateTotal` instead of `calc`. It is also helpful to write short functions that each perform a single task. This makes your code easier to test and reuse. Overall, writing clean code improves maintainability, meaning you or another developer can quickly make changes or fix bugs without spending hours trying to figure out how the code works.
